behavior_summary: U8P_GHOSTEGG implements egg lifecycle behavior: leaving a fast-area adjusts a stored egg state, and a helper process can convert the egg into a spawned NPC (U8P_GHOSTEGG::hatch).

U8P_GHOSTEGG::leaveFastArea: when the actor leaves the fast-area trigger, the script reads the egg id/state (Egg::getEggId). If the id >= 0x64 (100) it subtracts 0x64 and writes the result back (Egg::setEggId), effectively toggling/decrementing a U8P_GHOSTEGG::hatch-related state tied to area exit.

U8P_GHOSTEGG::hatch: if the egg is not already in the >=0x64 state it may (depending on id checks and a probabilistic urandom test) set the egg id to 0x64, create an NPC (Npc::create), apply an item status bit (Item::orStatus), place/pop the spawn at the egg's coordinates (Item::pop with Item::getZ/Y/X), configure orientation/initial action (spawn->METHOD::func15E7 using Item::getDirToItem), set target/alignment (Npc::setTarget, Npc::setAlignment) and force it into combat (Npc::setInCombat).
notable_control_flow: U8P_GHOSTEGG::leaveFastArea: single conditional branch — only acts when Egg::getEggId(this) >= 0x64.

U8P_GHOSTEGG::hatch: guarded by Egg::getEggId(this) checks. One branch short-circuits if id >= 0x64. Another branch compares id != 0x01 and performs a probabilistic gate (urandom(0x0A) <= 0x08) before proceeding to spawn, so hatching can be random/conditional.
U8P_GHOSTEGG::hatch performs a sequence of state write (Egg::setEggId), spawn creation and world placement/configuration when the gating conditions pass.
open_questions: What concrete meanings map to egg id values (0x01, 0x64, and values >=0x64)? These are inferred as state markers but exact semantics (species, stage, or flag bits) are unknown.
When and how is U8P_GHOSTEGG::hatch invoked in gameplay (timer, use, damage, or other event)? The process type suggests an item process but the entry trigger is not present in this excerpt.
Are there other systems that rely on the egg id arithmetic (subtract 0x64 on exit) for multi-state toggles or paired-area behavior? The broader interaction with other scripts is not shown.

main_function.name: U8P_GHOSTEGG::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: On leaving a fast-area trigger, adjust the egg's stored id/state by subtracting 0x64 when the egg id is >= 0x64 — likely to clear or decrement a U8P_GHOSTEGG::hatch-related flag set while inside the area.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine when the actor leaves a defined fast-area region (U8P_GHOSTEGG::leaveFastArea event).
Runtime condition: Egg::getEggId(this) >= 0x64 is required for action to occur.

function_map.name: U8P_GHOSTEGG::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: fast-area exit handler that adjusts egg state by subtracting 0x64 when current id >= 0x64

function_map.name: U8P_GHOSTEGG::hatch
function_map.role: attempt to U8P_GHOSTEGG::hatch/transform the egg into an NPC: sets egg id, spawns and configures NPC and places it at the egg's coordinates (probabilistic and id-checked)

external_calls.purpose: Read the egg's stored id/state to decide whether to subtract 0x64
Write the updated egg id/state after subtracting 0x64 on area exit
Check current egg id/state to gate U8P_GHOSTEGG::hatch behavior (compares against 0x64 and 0x01)
Provide a random value for probabilistic U8P_GHOSTEGG::hatch gate (e.g., urandom(0x0A) <= 0x08)
Mark egg as hatched/changed by setting id to 0x64 before spawning
Instantiate the NPC that the egg hatches into (returns reference stored in local1)
Apply a status bitmask (0x0080) to the created NPC/item, likely to mark it special or non-interactable as part of spawn
Place/pop the created NPC/item at the egg's world coordinates using Item::getZ/Y/X(this)
Finalize orientation or issue an initial action on the spawned NPC using Item::getDirToItem and a flags argument
Set the spawned NPC's target (likely the player/party) so it will engage
Set spawned NPC alignment/faction (0x40) to affect hostility/relations
Place the spawned NPC into combat state so it will act/attack immediately

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_GHOSTEGG implements egg lifecycle behavior: leaving a fast-area adjusts a stored egg state, and a helper process can convert the egg into a spawned NPC (U8P_GHOSTEGG::hatch).
